title: Preparation of Lipopolysaccharide for Intraperitoneal Injection
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cria4","text":"This protocol describes the steps for preparing 20X stock and experimental solution of Lipopolysaccharide (LPS) intended for intraperitoneal injections in mice at a concentration of 0.25 mg/mL. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"focam","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"rzsc7","text":"LPS can be given at dose 0.1 to 2.5 mg/kg in saline via intraperitoneal injection at a max volume of 0.5 mL, for a maximum of 1 day.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"66hmr","text":"Proper PPE includes lab coat, eye protection, and disposable gloves. Report any accidental sticks to EHS. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"52r8o","text":"When handling syringes with needles, always ensure needles are pointed away from you to avoid any accidental sticks. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d7c3g","text":"Used needles and syringes should be disposed of in biohazard sharps container. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"foea0","text":"Lipopolysaccharide (LPS) is a potent immune stimulant and should be prepared in a fume hood and under sterile conditions with proper PPE. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Upon arrival of 5 mg Lipopolysaccharide (LPS) powder, in a fume hood dissolve in 1 mL of pharmaceutical grade sterile saline (0.9%) to make a 5 mg/mL stock solution. Note: If stock solution isn’t prepared upon arrival, store LPS powder at 4 °C .
Swab surface of sterile saline bottle septum with a 70% ethanol swab.
Eject saline in syringe into the bottle containing 5 mg LPS powder.
Mix the contents for ~ 00:01:00 by inverting and shaking the bottle to ensure all powder on the bottle's interior surface is dissolved.
Pipette 25 µL aliquots of stock solution in low protein binding tubes and store at -20 °C . Stock solutions can be stored at -20°C for approximately 2 years before expiring.
Remove label from the empty LPS bottle and discard bottle in biohazard waste.
Place label on clipboard that documents used chemicals in the lab.
At most 01:00:00 before the injection on the experimental day, dilute stock by 20X (to 0.25 mg/mL final concentration).
Rapidly thaw stock aliquot by holding fingers to the wall of the tube for about 00:01:00 .
Add 475 µL of saline and mix by inverting the tube or by vortexing.
Leftover LPS should be discarded in a biohazard waste container.
creator: Avery Weaver
